steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: Support-the-claim question. The student's claim has two parts: (1) obedience to law in a healthy state must be as close to absolute as possible, and (2) even minor infractions should not be ignored. The correct quotation must address minor or unnoticed violations accumulating to harm the state.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Transgression creeps in unperceived and at last ruins the state...frequent small expenditures slowly...chipping away at a fortune': directly supports both parts — small/unnoticed infractions, if tolerated, accumulate until they ruin the state, so they cannot be ignored. ✓
  • Choice B — 'magistrates cannot possibly make money...oligarchies...susceptible to corruption through bribery': about preventing financial corruption of officials, not about strict enforcement against minor citizen infractions. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Constitutions are preserved when their destroyers are at a distance...fear of them': about external threats motivating vigilance, not about treating every minor infraction as significant. ✗
  • Choice D — 'oligarchies may last...because the rulers are on good terms...with the unenfranchised': about power-sharing in oligarchies to maintain stability, unrelated to absolute obedience or minor infractions. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
